title: Exceptions should not be created without being thrown
description: Exceptions should not be created without being thrownAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearreliabilityBugerror-handlingWhy is this an issue?More InfoCreating a newExceptionwithout actually throwing does not achieve the intended purpose.if (x < 0) { new ArgumentException("x must be nonnegative"); }Ensure to throw theExceptionwith athrowstatement.if (x < 0) { throw new ArgumentException("x must be nonnegative"); }Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab repositoriesAnalyze code in youron-premise CI©2025SonarSource Sàrl. All rights reserved.Privacy Policy|Cookie Policy|Terms of Use
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Exception created but not thrown - use 'throw' keyword or assign to variable

never_trigger_on: Exception assignment to variables (var ex = new Exception)
Unit tests creating exceptions for testing
logic.reasoning: Detects 'new Exception()' statements that are not preceded by 'throw' keyword and not assigned to variables or returned. This catches cases where exceptions are instantiated but never thrown, which is a logic error. Excludes assignments, returns, throw statements, and test code.

should_trigger: if (x < 0) { new ArgumentException("x must be nonnegative"); }
should_not_trigger: if (x < 0) { throw new ArgumentException("x must be nonnegative"); }

logic.self_critique_notes: Pattern uses negative lookbehind to ensure 'throw' doesn't precede 'new Exception'. Added exclusions for assignments (var/field = new Exception), return statements, and actual throw statements. Edge cases considered: (1) Exception in variable assignment - excluded via assignment patterns; (2) Exception as return value - excluded via return pattern; (3) Test code creating exceptions for assertions - excluded via test attributes.
